titleStmt.title: Belmont, MA, Alwan Collection, Alwan Codex 40
Alwan Codex 40

editionStmt.p: The initial version of this file was created from data kindly provided by the Ethiopic Manuscript Imaging Project directed by Steve Delamarter.

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

licence.p: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

msContents.summary: Homiliary in Honor of the Archangel Michael, ድርሳነ፡ ሚካኤል፡

msItem.title: Homily by Dämatewos, bishop of Alexandria
Synaxary Entry: Mission of the Archangel to Joshua, son of Nun
Synaxary Entry: Mission of the Archangel to rescue the three holy children
Synaxary Entry: Mission of the Archangel to save Jacob from the wrath of Esau
Synaxary Entry: Mission of the Archangel to Samson, the giant
Homily by the archbishop of Antioch
Synaxary Entry: Mission of the Archangel to Balaam
Synaxary entries: Mission of the Archangel to the Prophet Jeremiah
Homily by Yoḥannǝs, Bishop of Ethiopia, who came after Bishop Yǝsḥäq, on the conflict between the consort of King Arqadewos and John Chysostom
Synaxary entry: Mission of the Archangel to Habakkuk
Homily by Yoḥannǝs, Bishop of Aksum on the family of Astäraniqos and Euphemia
Synaxary entry: The building of the church of the Archangel in place of a pagan temple in Alexandria
Homily [by Bishop Yoḥannǝs] on the wicked wealthy man whose property was inherited by the son of his poor neighbor
Synaxary entry: Mission to the camp of Sennacherib
Homily (anonymous) on the book of the angels that came from Jerusalem
Synaxary entry: Mission of the Archangel to the Emperor Constantine
Homily (anonymous) on not worshipping other gods
Synaxary entry: Mission to Prophet Isaiah
Homily (anonymous) on the need to make the angels sureties by honoring them
Synaxary entry: Mission to Prophet Samuel

collation.note: Quire descriptions: protection quire and quires 1–12 and 15–16 balanced; quire 13–14 and 17–18 adjusted balanced.

list.item: 7 Fols 97–103 A folio stub is visible between fols. 101, 102. Quire 13: 3, stub after 5
7 Fols 104–110 A folio stub is visible between fols. 109, 110. Quire 14: 2, stub after 6
7 Fols 127–133 A folio stub is visible between fols. 130, 131. Quire 17: 4, stub before 5
5 Fols 134–138 A folio stub is visible between fols. 137, 138. Quire 18: 2, stub after 4

handNote.desc: The words Michael, Mary, God, and Gabriel are rubricated

decoNote.desc: Holy Man Portrait,St George of Lydda and the
Angel Michael with wings outstretched and a lesser on either side
Patron devoted to Holy Man Portrait Michael facing the illumination on fol. 13r. Owner’s name deleted
Angel Michael enthroned holding a processional and handkerchiefwith caption “How Saint Michael, the Archangel, was enthroned.”

Angel Michael and the three young men in the furnace. The Gabriel stands with a processional behind. In painting the icon with both s in it, the painter has reconciled the contradiction between the Dersana Gabriel which claims the was Gabriel, and the Synaxarium that claims that it was Michael
Holy Man Portrait Michael rescues ያዕቆብ፡ from Esau
Scene of the combat of Holy Man Portrait Michael. Many people stand under his wings. A demon with horns is in a fire at the bottom of the page
Angel Michael and the giant, Samson
Angel Michael with Balaam and the Donkey
Angel Michael delivers Jeremiah from jail. Ebed-Melech kneels at Michael’s feet
Angel Michael transports Habakkuk to Daniel to bring him food in the lion’s den
Angel Michael protecting Baḥran
Angel Michael assists a bishop to cast out a demon
Hezekiah looks to the Angel for healing
Angel Michael heals a blind man at a church

decoDesc.decoNote: Fol. 130vPatron, Abba Bäğğä, offers up necklace to Angel Michael who stand over a woman lying before him

decoNote.desc: Holy Man Portrait surrounded by creatures, bird, and a man (the patron, Abba Bäğğä) offering a necklace
Angel Michael at the Day of Judgment
Angel Michael and the Drowning of the Egyptian soldiers in the Red Sea
Holy Man Portrait,St George of Lydda and the
Virgin and Child, with patron, Abba Bäğğä, prostrate before them
Crucifixion of Jesus The name of the artist, represented taking refuge under the cross, is Fänta. With caption “How Fänta begged.”
The Trinity surrounded by the four living creatures
Lion of Judah standing on a slain ox

item.desc: Fol. 36r Hymn with which the Synaxary is concluded
Fol. ir(ecto) Record on the return of loaned money (12 birr), with interest (total of 50 birr), deposited with a friend
f. iir(ecto), col. 1 Record of a reconciliation between two people
f. iir(ecto), col. 2 Record of income of the church
Fol. 22r Prayer for Gäbrä Yoḥannǝs, replacing original owner’s name
Fol. 42r The name of the original owner, Kǝnfä Mika’el

f. 137v, col. 1 Record of Emancipation for Wälättä Yoḥannǝs. Text: አቶ ምሕረቱ ወለተ ዮሐንስን ወይዘሮ ዘሪቱ ወለተ ዮሐንስን አርነት ሲአወጡ ዳኞች ተገኘ ተሰማ አቶ አላባ አቶ የሽታ ናቸው ዋሶች የአቶ ምሕረት ዋሶች ዓለሙ መሸሻ ቄስ የተመኙ ናቸው ብላታ ተገኘ የወልተ ዮሐንስ ፍጅ ሁኖ አርነት ሲአሶጣት ዋሶቹ ወርቅነህ ንጉሤ አልማው ገብርየ ናቸው እዳድ ልጅ ልታጠባ ርስት ከብት ልትካፈል ነው
f. 137v, col. 2 Notice of power of attorney

binding.decoNote: Parchment, 270 x 220 x 75 mm, four Coptic chain stitches attached with bridle attachments to rough hewn wooden boards covered with tooled leather, decorative linen covers the tooled leater with a linen strip to surround the folios, headband and tailband.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

t:revisionDesc.change: Created XML record from EMIP Collection Metadata.xsls
Added quire desc, binding desc, margin dim, columns/lines, dating info
